exemplar.title: Prompt 27: Does all knowledge impose ethical obligations on those who know it?

detailedExemplarData.title: Prompt 27: Does all knowledge impose ethical obligations on those who know it?

strengths: Three specific, well-contextualized objects are clearly identified with dates, provenance and publication details
Effective use of the COINTELPRO memorandum to illustrate ethical imperatives, with named frameworks to support analysis
Strong real-world grounding for the saviour siblings article, linking bioethical principles such as principlism to the prompt
Engaging visual of Nietzscheâs Beyond Good and Evil anchors the third object and adds depth to the discussion of perspectivism
weaknesses: Explanations of links to the prompt sometimes drift into description and lack precision, with some repetition
Conflation of distinct Nietzschean concepts weakens the clarity of the ethical argument
Bibliographic inconsistencies (use of SparkNotes, duplicated entries, misformatted table) undermine academic rigor
Some claimsâe.g. on human rights violations and utilitarian experimentsâare asserted rather than supported by specific evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission aligns with the 5â6 band: three well-chosen objects are clearly presented and linked to the IA prompt, with coherent justifications and multiple citations. However, uneven precision in the explanations, occasional descriptive drift, and inconsistent sourcing prevent it from achieving the consistently well-supported, precise analysis required for a 7 or above. A score of 5 accurately reflects these strengths and areas for improvement.

annotations.comment: The clear and prominently placed exhibition title helps orient the reader and establishes context immediately.
The exhibition clearly states the chosen IA prompt, providing a focused TOK question to guide the objects. This strong alignment sets a precise basis for analysis.
To enhance coherence, consider adding a concise introduction or roadmap after the prompt outlining the three objects and their ethical dimensions.
The student precisely labels and isolates the first object, aiding clarity in structure and flow.
The provenance and date of the FBI memorandum are clearly provided, demonstrating strong contextual grounding for Object 1.
Analysis drifts into descriptive recounting and contains repetition; focus more on deepening the link between the memorandum and the ethical prompt.
Refine the ethical frameworks discussion by explicitly contrasting care ethics and virtue ethics in relation to the prompt rather than just naming them.
Could the student explain why care ethics is more pertinent than other moral theories in assessing FBI agentsâ obligations?
The student effectively uses the investigation and condemnation of COINTELPRO to underscore ethical imperatives, strengthening the argument.
Claims about human rights violations would benefit from specific examples or citations to reinforce the ethical consequences described.
Integrate a clearer link back to the prompt by explicitly stating how the governmentâs response illustrates the ethical obligations of those with knowledge.
The context of the article and introduction of Adam Nash provides a compelling real-world grounding for Object 2.
Publication details such as date and author should be stated in-text to strengthen the objectâs specificity and context.
The table appears empty or misformatted; clarify its purpose or remove it to avoid confusion.
Further develop the connection between Kantian ethics and the saviour siblings dilemma by explaining why human dignity is central to the prompt.
The exploration of principlism and bioethical values adds depth, linking the object to broader ethical frameworks.
The brief mention of utilitarianism experiments lacks clear explanation of its relevance; expand to show explicit ethical implications.
Cite specific components of principlism by referencing primary bioethics literature to strengthen evidence for ethical obligations.
Including an image of Nietzscheâs book visually anchors Object 3 and enhances reader engagement with the physical artifact.
The discussion remains abstract; integrate edition-specific details (publication year, translation) to justify the selection of this copy.
Incorporate direct quotations from Beyond Good and Evil to illustrate how Nietzscheian perspectivism challenges universal ethical obligations.
The analysis of the Ãbermensch concept effectively challenges the idea of universal ethical obligations, showing nuanced understanding.
The section on moral absolutism and perspectivism conflates distinct Nietzschean concepts; refine distinctions for greater precision.
Clarify how resentment of the weak against the strong directly relates to the ethical prompt rather than remaining descriptive of Nietzscheâs views.
The bibliography is prominently titled and demarcated, ensuring clear separation from the analytical content.
Including SparkNotes as a source undermines academic rigor; replace secondary summaries with primary text or scholarly analysis.
Bibliographic entries exhibit inconsistent formatting (e.g., author order, date placement); apply a standard citation style throughout.
The duplication of the Virtue Ethics reference from previous pages is redundant; consolidate and avoid repetition in the bibliography.
Ensure that web resources are accompanied by access dates and consistent URL formatting to improve citation quality.
Cross-check footnotes with bibliography to guarantee all cited sources are listed and formatted uniformly.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content:
The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
